name: CSRF middleware removed from Express app
cve_reference: CWE-352 / OWASP CSRF — pattern documented in countless OSS Express advisories, e.g. CVE-2024-39014 (csurf-removal regression in OSS apps).
pattern_description: Removal of `csurf` middleware on the assumption that 'SPA uses Authorization header so CSRF is not relevant'. This is partially true (token-bearer flows are CSRF-immune) but ignores cookie-authenticated routes, mixed-mode auth, and 3rd-party callbacks. The default-on protection should be replaced explicitly per-route, not stripped globally.
notes: If the app uses cookies anywhere (login, session, OAuth state), this regression re-introduces CSRF. The 'SPA' justification in the comment is a strong heuristic that the change was made without auditing cookie-auth endpoints. NEEDS_HUMAN verdict is acceptable because the diff alone cannot confirm whether any cookie-auth route remains.
